content.problemStatement:
Create a Python program that validates RGB color codes. The program should take a string input representing an RGB color code and validate if it's in the correct format. The correct format for an RGB color code is a string of 6 hexadecimal digits (0-9 or A-F, case-insensitive).

You need to implement a custom exception called `InvalidRGBColorError` that will be raised when the input is not a valid RGB color code. The program should use this custom exception to handle invalid inputs.

If the input is valid, the program should return the uppercase version of the color code. If it's invalid, it should raise the `InvalidRGBColorError` with an appropriate error message.

Example:
Input: "FF00AB"
Output: "FF00AB"

Input: "GH12K4"
Output: InvalidRGBColorError: Invalid RGB color code. Must be 6 hexadecimal digits.
constraints: A valid code consists of exactly 6 characters from 0‑9, A‑F, a‑f
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 1000), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a string S representing an RGB color code. The line may have leading or trailing spaces which must be ignored.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line. If S is a valid RGB color code (exactly 6 hexadecimal digits, case‑insensitive) print the uppercase version of S. Otherwise print the exact error message: "Invalid RGB color code. Must be 6 hexadecimal digits."

sampleTestCases.output: FF00AB\nInvalid RGB color code. Must be 6 hexadecimal digits.
sampleTestCases.explanation: First line is a valid 6‑digit hexadecimal code, so it is printed in uppercase. Second line contains characters G and K which are not hexadecimal, therefore the error message is printed.
The code consists of six valid hexadecimal digits; converting to uppercase yields 00FF00.

sampleTestCases.output: Invalid RGB color code. Must be 6 hexadecimal digits.
sampleTestCases.explanation: After trimming the line the length is 5, not 6, so it is invalid.

hidden.label: empty after trim
hidden.expectedOutput: Invalid RGB color code. Must be 6 hexadecimal digits.

hidden.label: mixed valid and invalid
hidden.expectedOutput: FFAA11\nInvalid RGB color code. Must be 6 hexadecimal digits.\n00FF00

hidden.label: various cases
hidden.expectedOutput: ABCDEF\n123456\nInvalid RGB color code. Must be 6 hexadecimal digits.

solution.approach: For each test case read the whole line, strip leading and trailing whitespace and obtain the candidate string s. If s.length != 6 the code is invalid. Otherwise check every character: it must be a hexadecimal digit (0‑9, a‑f, A‑F). If any character fails, the code is invalid. If all checks pass, output s converted to uppercase. The algorithm is O(1) per test case because at most six characters are examined, so total time is O(T). Only a few variables are stored, giving O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case examines at most six characters.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only constant‑size variables are used.
